description_long: This is a roughly cylindrical stone fragment with an irregular, jagged top and a more angular, broken base. The surface displays a mix of gray, white, and brown tones, with dark mineral veins and patches of weathered rock. One side features a series of black, hand-painted or marker-written numbers or letters, suggesting it may have been cataloged or marked for identification. The stone's texture is coarse and uneven, with visible chips, cracks, and natural layering. Its overall appearance suggests it was extracted from a larger formation, possibly as a core sample or barrier stone. The weathering and coloration indicate prolonged exposure to outdoor elements.
description: A jagged, cylindrical stone fragment with gray, white, and brown mottling, featuring black markings and a rough, weathered surface.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the stone's elongated profile, with visible black markings and a mix of gray and brown coloration.
description_view_1: A broader face with prominent black writing, mottled gray and white stone, and rough, chipped edges.
description_view_2: The opposite side, showing a darker, more uniform surface with less visible writing and a pronounced angular break.
description_view_3: The back view reveals a blockier, more angular surface with brown and gray patches and less visible text.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a barrier along a curb or path
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and veining with patches of dark mineral and hand-applied black markings; no regular artificial pattern.
attribute_weathering: Rough and chipped with visible cracks and discoloration, indicating moderate weathering and outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or concrete surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; likely stands out due to markings.
